description_long: This object is a compact electronic device with a green printed circuit board (PCB) exposed, featuring various electronic components such as chips, capacitors, and connectors. The PCB is partially surrounded by a metallic or plastic casing that appears warped or incomplete, revealing the internal structure. Several ports are visible on one side, likely for power, audio, or data connections, and there are copper heat sinks attached to some of the chips for thermal management. The surface of the PCB is marked with white silkscreen text, including a Raspberry Pi logo and HDMI labeling, indicating it is a Raspberry Pi-based radio receiver. The object is small, lightweight, and would typically be found in a DIY electronics project or as part of a custom radio receiver setup in an urban environment. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with exposed circuitry and minimal external finishing.
description: A small, exposed green circuit board with visible electronic components, ports, and partial casing, resembling a Raspberry Pi-based radio receiver.
description_view_0: The main face of the device is visible, showing the green PCB, copper heat sinks, and several ports on the right side.
description_view_1: A narrow side view, mostly showing the edge of the PCB and the warped casing, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: The opposite face of the device, again showing the PCB and ports, but from a slightly different angle, highlighting the HDMI label and more of the casing.
description_view_3: Another narrow side view, showing the edge of the PCB and the casing, with some texture detail on the casing's surface.
